2717	\begin{definition}\leanok
2718	[The morphism induced by a rank-\(d\) quotient]
2719	  \label{def:grPointOfRankQuotient}
2720	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.grPointOfRankQuotient}
2721	  \uses{lem:chartLocus_isOpenCover, lem:isIso_pullback_isoLocus_map,
2722	    def:gr_affine_chart, def:gr_chart_quotient, def:gr_transition, lem:gr_cocycle,
2723	    def:gr_glued_scheme, def:gr_chartComposite, def:gr_chartMatrix,
2724	    def:gr_chartMorphism, lem:gr_chartMorphism_glue_compat}
2725	  Let \(x = \langle \mathcal{F}, q\rangle\) be a rank-\(d\) quotient of
2726	  \(\mathcal{O}_T^{\,r}\) on a scheme \(T\) (\cref{def:gr_rankQuotient}). The morphism
2727	  \[
2728	    \mathrm{grPointOfRankQuotient}(x) \;:\; T \;\longrightarrow\; \mathrm{Gr}(r,d)
2729	  \]
2730	  is constructed as follows. Over each chart locus \(T_I = \mathrm{chartLocus}(x, I)\)
2731	  (\cref{def:chartLocus}) the chart composite \(s_I \circ q\) is an isomorphism
2732	  (\cref{lem:isIso_pullback_isoLocus_map}), so trivialising \(\mathcal{F}|_{T_I}\) by its
2733	  inverse presents \(q|_{T_I}\) as a \(d \times r\) matrix \(M^I\) of global functions on
2734	  \(T_I\) whose \(I\)-minor is the identity. Its \(d(r-d)\) complementary entries determine a
2735	  unique \(\mathbb{Z}\)-algebra homomorphism \(R^I = \mathbb{Z}[X^I] \to \Gamma(T_I,
2736	  \mathcal{O}_{T_I})\), \(x^I_{p,q} \mapsto M^I_{p,q}\), hence by the \(\Gamma\)--\(\Spec\)
2737	  adjunction a unique \emph{chart morphism} \(\varphi_I : T_I \to U^I\)
2738	  (\cref{def:gr_affine_chart}) with \(\varphi_I^{*}X^I = M^I\), and thus
2739	  \(\varphi_I^{*}u^I = q|_{T_I}\) (\cref{def:gr_chart_quotient}). The chart loci cover \(T\)
2740	  (\cref{lem:chartLocus_isOpenCover}), and on each overlap \(T_I \cap T_J\) the two
2741	  presenting matrices are change-of-basis related by the invertible \(I\)-columns minor,
2742	  \(M^J = (M^I_J)^{-1} M^I = \varphi_I^{*}\theta_{I,J}(X^J)\)
2743	  (\cref{def:gr_transition}); hence the composites \(T_I \xrightarrow{\varphi_I} U^I
2744	  \hookrightarrow \mathrm{Gr}(r,d)\) with the chart immersions agree on overlaps after the
2745	  transition \(\theta_{I,J}\). By the cocycle condition (\cref{lem:gr_cocycle}) these
2746	  chart-local morphisms glue to a single morphism \(T \to \mathrm{Gr}(r,d)\)
2747	  (\cref{def:gr_glued_scheme}); this glued morphism is
2748	  \(\mathrm{grPointOfRankQuotient}(x)\), the local-to-global inverse of the universal
2749	  property: pulling the tautological quotient back along it recovers \(\langle \mathcal{F},
2750	  q\rangle\) up to equivalence.
2751	\end{definition}
2752	
2753	The two blocks below carry the chart data of a quotient through a base change
2754	\(\psi : T' \to T\); they are the bridge underlying the two inverse laws of the
2755	representability theorem.
2756	
2757	\begin{lemma}[Chart composite of a pulled-back rank-\(d\) quotient]
2758	  \label{lem:gr_chartComposite_rqPullback}
2759	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.chartComposite_rqPullback}
2760	  \uses{def:gr_chartComposite, def:gr_rankQuotient, lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso_inv_freeMap}
2761	  For a morphism \(\psi : T' \to T\) and a rank-\(d\) quotient \(y\) on \(T\), the chart
2762	  composite of the pulled-back quotient \(\psi^{*}y\) (\cref{def:gr_rankQuotient}) is the
2763	  \(\psi\)-pullback of the chart composite of \(y\), conjugated through the free-pullback
2764	  comparison:
2765	  \[
2766	    s_I \circ (\psi^{*}y)
2767	      \;=\; Q_d^{-1} \circ \psi^{*}(s_I \circ y).
2768	  \]
2769	\end{lemma}
2770	\begin{proof}
2771	  \uses{lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso_inv_freeMap}
2772	  Unfolding the pullback action, \(s_I \circ (\psi^{*}y)\) is the coordinate inclusion
2773	  \(s_I\) followed by \(Q_r^{-1}\) and \(\psi^{*}y\). The inverse free-pullback comparison
2774	  intertwines the index inclusion (\cref{lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso_inv_freeMap}): sliding
2775	  \(s_I\) across \(Q_r^{-1}\) turns it into \(Q_d^{-1}\) followed by \(\psi^{*}(s_I)\),
2776	  whence \(s_I \circ (\psi^{*}y) = Q_d^{-1} \circ \psi^{*}(s_I \circ y)\).
2777	\end{proof}
2778	
2779	\begin{lemma}[The chart locus of a pullback contains the preimage of the chart locus]
2780	  \label{lem:gr_chartLocus_rqPullback}
2781	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.chartLocus_rqPullback}
2782	  \uses{def:chartLocus, lem:gr_chartComposite_rqPullback, lem:gr_isIso_pullback_map_comp,
2783	    def:modules_pullbackComp}
2784	  For \(\psi : T' \to T\) and a rank-\(d\) quotient \(y\) on \(T\), the \(\psi\)-preimage of
2785	  the \(I\)-chart locus of \(y\) is contained in the \(I\)-chart locus of the pulled-back
2786	  quotient:
2787	  \[
2788	    \psi^{-1}\bigl(\mathrm{chartLocus}(y, I)\bigr)
2789	      \;\le\; \mathrm{chartLocus}(\psi^{*}y, I).
2790	  \]
2791	\end{lemma}
2792	\begin{proof}
2793	  \uses{lem:gr_chartComposite_rqPullback, lem:gr_isIso_pullback_map_comp,
2794	    def:modules_pullbackComp}
2795	  Let \(U = \psi^{-1}(\mathrm{chartLocus}(y, I))\). The inclusion \(U \hookrightarrow T'\)
2796	  followed by \(\psi\) factors through the chart-locus inclusion
2797	  \(\iota : \mathrm{chartLocus}(y, I) \hookrightarrow T\), over which the chart composite
2798	  \(s_I \circ y\) pulls back to an isomorphism (\cref{def:chartLocus}). Hence its pullback
2799	  along the factoring composite is an isomorphism
2800	  (\cref{lem:gr_isIso_pullback_map_comp}), and the pullback pseudofunctor comparison
2801	  (\cref{def:modules_pullbackComp}) together with the chart-composite bridge
2802	  (\cref{lem:gr_chartComposite_rqPullback}) identifies it with the pullback to \(U\) of the
2803	  chart composite of \(\psi^{*}y\). So \(s_I \circ (\psi^{*}y)\) is invertible over \(U\),
2804	  i.e.\ \(U\) is one of the opens whose supremum defines
2805	  \(\mathrm{chartLocus}(\psi^{*}y, I)\); taking the supremum gives
2806	  \(U \le \mathrm{chartLocus}(\psi^{*}y, I)\).
2807	\end{proof}
2808	
2809	\begin{theorem}
2810	
2811	\leanok
2812	[\(\mathrm{Gr}(r,d)\) represents the Grassmannian functor]
2813	  \label{thm:grassmannian_universal_property}
2814	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.represents}
2815	  \uses{def:grassmannian_functor, def:tautological_quotient,
2816	    def:gr_universal_quotient_sheaf, def:gr_glued_scheme, def:gr_affine_chart,
2817	    def:gr_universal_matrix, def:gr_transition, lem:gr_cocycle}
2818	  % SOURCE: [Nitsure], "Construction of Hilbert and Quot Schemes", \S 1,
2819	  % Exercise (2) "Grassmannian as a Quot scheme" (read from
2820	  % references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex, L546-L566).
2821	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
2822	  % "Show that $\grass(r,d)$ together with the quotient
2823	  %  $u:\oplus^r\,{\mathcal O}_{\grass(r,d)} \to \U$ represents the
2824	  %  contravariant functor
2825	  %  $$\Grass(r,d) = \Quot_{\oplus^r\,{\mathcal O}_{\Z}/\Z/\Z}^{d, {\mathcal O}_{\Z}}$$
2826	  %  from schemes to sets, which associates to any $T$ the
2827	  %  set of all equivalence classes
2828	  %  $\langle \F, q\rangle$ of quotients $q: \oplus^r\,{\mathcal O}_T \to \F$
2829	  %  where $\F$ is a locally free sheaf on $T$ of rank $d$.
2830	  %  Therefore, $\quot_{\oplus^r\,{\mathcal O}_{\Z}/\Z/\Z}^{d, {\mathcal O}_{\Z}}$
2831	  %  exists, and equals $\grass(r,d)$."
2832	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S 1, Exercise (2).}
2833	  The Grassmannian scheme \(\mathrm{Gr}(r,d)\) (\cref{def:gr_glued_scheme}),
2834	  together with the tautological quotient \(\langle \mathcal{U}, u \rangle\)
2835	  (\cref{def:tautological_quotient}), represents the functor
2836	  \(\operatorname{Grass}(r,d)\) (\cref{def:grassmannian_functor}). That is, for
2837	  every scheme \(T\) the natural map
2838	  \[
2839	    \operatorname{Hom}(T, \mathrm{Gr}(r,d))
2840	      \;\xrightarrow{\;\sim\;}\;
2841	    \operatorname{Grass}(r,d)(T),
2842	    \qquad
2843	    \bigl(\,\varphi : T \to \mathrm{Gr}(r,d)\,\bigr)
2844	      \;\longmapsto\;
2845	    \langle \varphi^{*}\mathcal{U},\, \varphi^{*}u \rangle,
2846	  \]
2847	  is a bijection, natural in \(T\). Consequently the Quot scheme
2848	  \(\quot^{\,d,\,\mathcal{O}_{\mathbb{Z}}}_{\oplus^{r}\mathcal{O}_{\mathbb{Z}}/
2849	  \mathbb{Z}/\mathbb{Z}}\) of rank-\(d\) quotients of the trivial rank-\(r\) sheaf
2850	  exists and equals \(\mathrm{Gr}(r,d)\); the tautological quotient is the
2851	  universal family.
2852	\end{theorem}
2853	
2854	\begin{proof}
2855	  \uses{def:tautological_quotient, def:gr_universal_quotient_sheaf,
2856	    def:gr_chart_quotient, def:gr_affine_chart, def:gr_universal_matrix,
2857	    def:gr_transition, lem:gr_cocycle, def:gr_glued_scheme,
2858	    def:grassmannian_functor, lem:tautologicalQuotient_epi,
2859	    def:grPointOfRankQuotient, lem:chartLocus_isOpenCover, def:chartLocus,
2860	    lem:isIso_pullback_isoLocus_map, def:gr_tautologicalRankQuotient,
2861	    def:gr_chartMorphism, def:gr_universalQuotient_restrictionIso,
2862	    lem:gr_comp_chartMorphism, lem:gr_chartComposite_rqPullback,
2863	    lem:gr_chartLocus_rqPullback, lem:gr_chartMorphism_glue_compat}
2864	  Naturality of \(\varphi \mapsto \langle \varphi^{*}\mathcal{U}, \varphi^{*}u
2865	  \rangle\) is immediate: pullback is functorial and \(\varphi^{*}u\) is again a
2866	  surjection onto a rank-\(d\) locally free sheaf, so the assignment defines a
2867	  morphism of functors \(\operatorname{Hom}(-,\mathrm{Gr}(r,d)) \to
2868	  \operatorname{Grass}(r,d)\). It remains to show it is a bijection for each
2869	  \(T\); we exhibit an inverse, constructed Zariski-locally on \(T\) and glued.
2870	
2871	  \emph{From a quotient to a morphism.} Let \(\langle \mathcal{F}, q \rangle \in
2872	  \operatorname{Grass}(r,d)(T)\), so \(q : \mathcal{O}_T^{\,r} \twoheadrightarrow
2873	  \mathcal{F}\) with \(\mathcal{F}\) locally free of rank \(d\). For a size-\(d\)
2874	  subset \(I \subseteq \{1,\dots,r\}\) let \(T_I \subseteq T\) be the locus where
2875	  the composite
2876	  \[
2877	    \mathcal{O}_T^{\,d}
2878	      \overset{\ \iota_I\ }{\hookrightarrow} \mathcal{O}_T^{\,r}
2879	      \xrightarrow{\ q\ } \mathcal{F},
2880	  \]
2881	  with \(\iota_I\) the inclusion of the \(I\)-indexed coordinates, is an
2882	  isomorphism. This locus is open: in a local trivialisation of \(\mathcal{F}\)
2883	  the composite is a \(d \times d\) matrix and \(T_I\) is the non-vanishing locus
2884	  of its determinant. The \(T_I\) cover \(T\): at any point \(t\), the
2885	  \(d\)-dimensional fibre quotient \(q(t) : \kappa(t)^{r} \twoheadrightarrow
2886	  \mathcal{F}\otimes\kappa(t)\) is surjective, so some \(d\) of the \(r\) standard
2887	  basis vectors map to a basis of the \(d\)-dimensional fibre, i.e.\ \(t \in T_I\)
2888	  for that \(I\) (by Nakayama the isomorphism then holds in a neighbourhood).
2889	
2890	  On \(T_I\), trivialise \(\mathcal{F}\) using \(\iota_I^{*}\): the quotient
2891	  becomes a surjection \(q_I : \mathcal{O}_{T_I}^{\,r} \to \mathcal{O}_{T_I}^{\,d}\)
2892	  represented by a \(d \times r\) matrix \(M^I\) whose \(I\)-th minor \(M^I_I\) is
2893	  the identity \(1_{d \times d}\) -- exactly the shape of the universal matrix
2894	  \(X^I\) (\cref{def:gr_universal_matrix}). The \(d(r-d)\) free entries of \(M^I\)
2895	  are global functions on \(T_I\) and hence determine a unique
2896	  \(\mathbb{Z}\)-algebra homomorphism \(R^I = \mathbb{Z}[X^I] \to
2897	  \Gamma(T_I, \mathcal{O}_{T_I})\) sending \(x^I_{p,q}\) to the corresponding entry
2898	  of \(M^I\), i.e.\ a unique morphism \(\varphi_I : T_I \to U^I\)
2899	  (\cref{def:gr_affine_chart}) with \(\varphi_I^{*}X^I = M^I\) and thus
2900	  \(\varphi_I^{*}u^I = q_I\) (\cref{def:gr_chart_quotient}).
2901	
2902	  \emph{Gluing.} On the overlap \(T_I \cap T_J\) the two trivialisations differ by
2903	  the change-of-basis \((M^I_J)^{-1}\), which is exactly the bundle transition
2904	  \(g_{I,J} = (X^I_J)^{-1}\) of \(\mathcal{U}\)
2905	  (\cref{def:gr_universal_quotient_sheaf}); concretely
2906	  \(M^J = (M^I_J)^{-1} M^I = \varphi_I^{*}\theta_{I,J}(X^J)\)
2907	  (\cref{def:gr_transition}, \cref{def:gr_universalMinorInv}). Hence
2908	  \(\varphi_I\) and \(\varphi_J\) land in the overlap charts and agree there after
2909	  the transition \(\theta_{I,J}\) -- this is the overlap compatibility
2910	  \cref{lem:gr_chartMorphism_glue_compat} -- so by the cocycle condition
2911	  (\cref{lem:gr_cocycle}) the \(\varphi_I\) glue to a single morphism
2912	  \(\varphi = \mathrm{grPointOfRankQuotient}(\langle \mathcal{F}, q\rangle) :
2913	  T \to \mathrm{Gr}(r,d)\) (\cref{def:grPointOfRankQuotient},
2914	  \cref{def:gr_glued_scheme}). This morphism is the candidate inverse; that it
2915	  is a two-sided inverse is the content of the two laws below.
2916	
2917	  \emph{Existence (the law \(\mathrm{right\_inv}\)):
2918	  \(\varphi^{*}\langle \mathcal{U}, u\rangle \sim \langle \mathcal{F}, q\rangle\).}
2919	  The equivalence is checked over the chart cover \(\{T_I\}\). Over \(T_I\) the
2920	  morphism \(\varphi\) restricts to \(\varphi_I\) followed by the chart immersion
2921	  \(\iota_I\) (\cref{def:gr_chartMorphism}), and the restriction of the universal
2922	  quotient sheaf along \(\iota_I\) is identified with the free rank-\(d\) sheaf by
2923	  the chart restriction isomorphism
2924	  \(\phi_I : \iota_I^{*}\mathcal{U} \xrightarrow{\sim} \mathcal{O}_{U^I}^{\,d}\)
2925	  (\cref{def:gr_universalQuotient_restrictionIso}). Through this identification
2926	  \(\varphi^{*}u\) becomes \(\varphi_I^{*}u^I\), which by construction of the chart
2927	  morphism (\(\varphi_I^{*}X^I = M^I\)) equals \(q|_{T_I}\) in the
2928	  \(I\)-trivialisation (\cref{def:gr_chartMorphism}, \cref{def:gr_chart_quotient}).
2929	  The witnessing isomorphisms \(\varphi^{*}\mathcal{F} \cong \mathcal{F}\) so
2930	  produced agree on overlaps -- the class is determined by \(\ker q\), which the
2931	  trivialisations match -- and assemble to a single isomorphism over \(T\), giving
2932	  \(\varphi^{*}\langle \mathcal{U}, u\rangle \sim \langle \mathcal{F}, q\rangle\).
2933	
2934	  \emph{Uniqueness (the law \(\mathrm{left\_inv}\)):
2935	  \(\mathrm{grPointOfRankQuotient}(\psi^{*}\langle \mathcal{U}, u\rangle) = \psi\)
2936	  for every \(\psi : T \to \mathrm{Gr}(r,d)\).} It suffices to check the equality
2937	  after restricting to each chart locus
2938	  \(\mathrm{chartLocus}(\psi^{*}\langle \mathcal{U}, u\rangle, I)\) of the
2939	  pulled-back tautological quotient. Over such a locus the composite of its
2940	  inclusion with \(\psi\) factors through the \(\psi\)-preimage of the tautological
2941	  chart locus, which is contained in that locus by
2942	  \cref{lem:gr_chartLocus_rqPullback}; the chart-composite bridge
2943	  \cref{lem:gr_chartComposite_rqPullback} identifies the chart data of
2944	  \(\psi^{*}\langle \mathcal{U}, u\rangle\) with the \(\psi\)-pullback of the
2945	  tautological chart data, so the matrix presenting it there is the \(\psi\)-image
2946	  of the universal matrix \(X^I\). Hence the chart morphism classifying
2947	  \(\psi^{*}\langle \mathcal{U}, u\rangle\) over this locus coincides with the
2948	  restriction of \(\psi\) into the chart \(U^I\) (\cref{lem:gr_comp_chartMorphism}).
2949	  As the chart loci cover, the glued morphism
2950	  \(\mathrm{grPointOfRankQuotient}(\psi^{*}\langle \mathcal{U}, u\rangle)\) equals
2951	  \(\psi\) by the uniqueness clause of the glue-data universal property.
2952	
2953	  Existence and uniqueness exhibit \(\mathrm{grPointOfRankQuotient}\) as a two-sided
2954	  inverse of \(\varphi \mapsto \langle \varphi^{*}\mathcal{U}, \varphi^{*}u\rangle\),
2955	  so the latter is bijective.
2956	
2957	  Therefore \(\mathrm{Gr}(r,d)\) with \(\langle \mathcal{U}, u \rangle\) is a fine
2958	  moduli space: it represents \(\operatorname{Grass}(r,d)\), so
2959	  \(\quot^{\,d,\,\mathcal{O}_{\mathbb{Z}}}_{\oplus^{r}\mathcal{O}_{\mathbb{Z}}/
2960	  \mathbb{Z}/\mathbb{Z}}\) exists and equals \(\mathrm{Gr}(r,d)\).
2961	\end{proof}
